title: Sanitation: latrine use/maintenance + safe disposal of child feces
context: Safe sanitation and excreta disposal are critical WASH protective behaviors. Latrine use/maintenance and safe disposal of child feces (a frequently missed pathway) are medium-risk household-practice topics. These units require both WASH/public-health accuracy review and cultural/dignity review, because sanitation guidance can be stigmatizing or culturally inapt if not carefully framed. They build on the authoring pattern from content-006.
objective: Author, source-cite, and get both WASH domain and cultural/dignity review of WashUnit documents covering: (1) latrine use and maintenance (cleanliness, safe use, slab maintenance, pit/superstructure hygiene); and (2) safe disposal of child feces (key WASH promotion message — child feces are often not treated as human waste; guidance on safe disposal methods).
acceptanceCriteria: Every claim tied to a specific named authoritative source (WHO/UNICEF/Sphere) with retrieval date and verified license; no verbatim copied text; no logos/emblems; CI copy/similarity check passes
Units conform to the WashUnit schema: all required fields present, riskClass is medium, no clinical instruction, steps are plain-language per the style guide
Each unit reviewed and approved by a WASH/public-health domain reviewer distinct from the author for accuracy and scope compliance
Each unit additionally reviewed by a cultural/dignity reviewer for gender-sensitive, non-stigmatizing, and culturally appropriate framing; both reviews distinct from the author
PR-tied review-log entries written for both the accuracy review and the cultural/dignity review
CI schema validation, citation-coverage, no-self-approval, readability, and copy/similarity gates all pass
output: Committed, domain-reviewed and cultural/dignity-reviewed, source-cited WashUnit documents for latrine use/maintenance and safe disposal of child feces, with PR-tied review-log entries.
